feature_family_hints: Directional scan features: needed to detect nearest special-role tokens or obstacles along rows or columns
Contiguity features: to capture whether a cell is part of a contiguous run of empty or special-role cells
Support/obstacle features: to determine if a cell is directly above, below, or adjacent to a static support or obstacle
Edge/boundary features: to check if a cell is at or near the grid boundary
Adjacency features: to detect if a cell is adjacent to a drawn trace or moving token
Region features: to distinguish actions in top/bottom bands or other grid regions
First-available features: to identify if a cell is the first available in a row or column
Blocked-path features: to determine if a path from the action cell to a target is blocked by obstacles
Alignment features: to capture whether a cell is aligned with vertical or horizontal structures
Manhattan distance features: to measure proximity to special-role tokens
Global count features: to encode the number of special-role tokens present in the grid
Segment-edge features: to detect if a cell is at the edge of a contiguous segment
Relative-position features: to encode the position of the action cell relative to other tokens
Local neighborhood features: to summarize the configuration of cells in a small window around the action cell
Run-length features: to capture the length of contiguous runs of empty or special-role cells
action_choice_hints: The candidate cell is the nearest in its column to a specific role token above or below.
The candidate cell is in the same row as a contiguous segment of a particular role.
There exists an unbroken path from the candidate cell to the grid boundary in a given direction.
The candidate cell is adjacent to a static or immovable token.
The candidate cell is within K steps of a moving or advancing token.
The candidate cell is the first encountered along a direction before hitting an obstacle.
The candidate cell is part of a vertical or horizontal run of similar role tokens.
The candidate cell is in a region (e.g., top or bottom band) with a higher density of a specific role.
The candidate cell is not blocked by any obstacle in a given direction.
There exists at least one direction from the candidate cell with no obstacles within a certain range.
The candidate cell is at the edge of a contiguous segment of a particular role.
The candidate cell is the only one in its row or column with a certain property.
The candidate cell is adjacent to a drawn trace or previously visited cell.
The candidate cell is the closest to a moving token along a straight line.
The candidate cell is in a region where all neighboring cells share a specific property.
transition_hints: Selecting a cell containing an advancing token causes that token to move or change state.
Selecting a cell adjacent to a drawn trace extends the trace to the selected cell.
Selecting a cell with a moving token causes the token to advance in a specific direction.
Selecting a cell at the edge of a contiguous segment causes the segment to grow or shift.
Selecting a cell blocked by an obstacle prevents any state change.
Selecting a cell in a region with static tokens leaves those tokens unchanged.
Selecting a cell in a vertical or horizontal run may cause the entire run to update.
Selecting a cell with no special role results in no change.
Selecting a cell adjacent to a static token may cause a new token to appear.
Selecting a cell at the boundary may trigger a special transition for edge cases.

diversity_requirements: Include templates that capture both local (adjacent cell) and global (row/column/region) patterns.
Ensure templates cover both direction-based and region-based predicates.
placeholder_usage_limits: Limit the number of templates with more than two placeholders to avoid combinatorial explosion.
Restrict templates that require enumeration over all possible directions to a maximum of two per template set.
avoidances: Avoid templates that only check for the presence of any token without considering role.
Avoid templates that rely solely on absolute coordinates or grid positions.
Avoid templates that duplicate the same predicate schema with only minor variations.
